prompt:
You are a software requirements analyst.
You are given a list of software requirements with their IDs.

Your task is to identify which of the following software requirements are non-functional requirements (NFRs).

Definition:

Functional requirements describe what the system should do, such as features, behaviors, user actions, data operations, or business rules.
Non-functional requirements describe how the system should be, perform, operate, or be constrained. They include quality attributes, system properties, technical constraints, environmental constraints, and operational constraints.

Non-functional requirements include, but are not limited to:

Performance, such as response time, throughput, scalability, capacity, concurrency, or resource usage.
Security, such as access control, authentication, authorization, data protection, integrity, or protection against malicious input.
Maintainability, such as modifiability, configurability, extensibility, code quality, ease of updates, or system evolution.
Usability, such as ease of use, accessibility, learnability, user experience, or user-interface quality expectations.
Reliability, availability, robustness, or fault tolerance.
Compatibility, portability, interoperability, supported platforms, supported environments, or technology constraints.

Important guidelines:

Requirements describing constraints on supported environments, platforms, technologies, compatibility, portability, or interoperability should be considered NFRs.
Requirements about system qualities, system properties, user experience, reliability, availability, or operational constraints should be considered NFRs.
Requirements that only describe a concrete system function, user action, data operation, or business rule should be considered FRs.
If a requirement could reasonably be interpreted as either FR or NFR, classify it based on its primary concern. A behavior, feature, user action, or data operation should be classified as FR. A quality attribute, system property, or explicit constraint should be classified as NFR.
Do not classify a requirement as NFR only because it is vague or difficult to understand.

Instructions:
For each requirement, output exactly one line in the format:
REQ_ID: NFR
or
REQ_ID: FR
Use only the original requirement IDs from the input.
Do not include explanations, summaries, or any extra text.
Do not change the format or add punctuation.

Output format (STRICT):
R1: NFR
R2: FR
R3: NFR
...

Requirements:
1. [R204] The product must support Internet Explorer 5.5 and above.
2. [R205] The product must support Mozilla Firefox 1.0 and above.
3. [R206] The product shall interface with the Choice Parts System.  This provides the feed of recycled parts data.
4. [R207] The product shall run on the existing hardware for all environments.
5. [R208] The product shall adhere to the corporate Architecture guidelines
6. [R209] The product shall comply with corporate User Interface Guidelines
7. [R210] The product shall comply with corporate color scheme
8. [R211] The appearance of the product shall appear professional
9. [R212] The product shall be easy to use by Adjusters and Collision Estimators.  95% of Adjusters and Collision Estimators shall find the product easy to use.
10. [R213] The product shall increase productivity of Collision Estimators. 80% of the Collision Estimators shall agree their productivity has increase within 1 month of using the product.
11. [R214] Users shall feel satisfied using the product.  85% of all users will be satisfied with the product.
12. [R215] The product shall be easy to learn by Adjusters and Collision Estimators.  The product shall be learned with two days onsite training
13. [R216] The user shall easily locate instructions while using the product.  User help can be found within 90% of the system.
14. [R217] The search for recycled parts shall take no longer than 15 seconds.  The search results shall be returned in under 15 seconds.
15. [R218] The search for the preferred repair facility shall take no longer than 8 seconds.  The preferred repair facility is returned within 8 seconds
16. [R219] The recycled parts audit report shall be returned to the user within 10 seconds.  The audit report shall be returned within 10 seconds.
17. [R220] The preferred repair facility ratings shall be saved within 5 seconds. The save shall occur within 5 seconds.
18. [R221] The product shall adhere to the corporate online availability schedule.  The application is brought down only within 98% of the scheduled outages per the availability schedule.
19. [R222] The product shall achieve a 98% uptime. The product shall not fail more than 2% of the available online time.
20. [R223] The product shall be able to handle up to 1500 simultaneous users. The system shall perform with 1500 users as a time.
21. [R224] The average number of recycled parts records per day shall be 50 000.
22. [R225] The product shall be able to handle 10 000 concurrent users within 2 years of the initial launch.
23. [R226] For estimators  the product shall be able to be operated in a repair facility during dirty and noisy conditions.
24. [R227] The product shall interface with the ChoiceParts system once per day at approximately 1:00 AM.
25. [R228] The maintenance of the product will fall within the established corporate maintenance windows.
26. [R229] The product shall be supported using the corporate support center.
27. [R230] Product installations and upgrades shall be handled by the middleware technology team within established launch time frames.
28. [R231] Only adjusters can request recycled parts audit reports. No users without an adjuster role shall request recycled parts audits.
29. [R232] Only adjusters with a supervisor role can update preferred repair facility ratings. Users without the supervisor role cannot access the repair facility ratings.
30. [R233] Only collision estimators shall search for recycled parts.  Users without the collision estimator role shall not have access to the search for recycled parts.
31. [R234] Only valid data shall be entered into the system.  No invalid data shall be entered into the system.
32. [R235] One insurance company shall not be able to view another insurance companys claim data.
33. [R236] The product shall free of computer viruses.
34. [R237] The system shall prevent malicious attacks including denial of service.
35. [R238] The product must comply with Sarbanes-Oxley.
36. [R239] The product shall comply with the estimatics laws relating to recycled parts usage.
37. [R240] The product shall comply with insurance regulations regarding claims processing.
38. [R241] The user shall search for the preferred repair facility using vehicle  vehicle location  and radius in miles
39. [R242] The vehicle data shall include vehicle year  make  and model
40. [R243] The vehicle location shall include street address  city  state  and zipcode
41. [R244] The system shall resolve the zipcode for the street address  city  and state if zipcode is unknown.
42. [R245] The search radius shall be between 1 and 30 miles
43. [R246] The system shall locate the preferred repair facility with the highest ratings for the input criteria
44. [R247] The adjuster shall enter the preferred repair facility on the estimate assignment.
45. [R248] The adjuster shall be able to override the preferred repair facitlity on the estimate assignment.
46. [R249] The preferred repair facility shall be returned to the user.
47. [R250] The system shall return a list of repair facilities within the radius if the preferred repair facility cannot be determined.
48. [R251] The estimator shall search for available recycled parts using damaged vehicle parts information.
49. [R252] The estimator shall search for available recycled parts using a list of preferred parts suppliers.
50. [R253] The system shall search for available recycled parts for the supplied vehicle parts and suppliers.
51. [R254] The estimator shall search by zipcode with a radius of 30 miles if no parts are found for the preferred parts suppliers
52. [R255] The system shall retain the available recycled parts and suppliers returned from the search.
53. [R256] The available recycled parts information and their suppliers shall be returned to the user.
54. [R257] The estimator shall apply selected recycled parts to the collision estimate.
55. [R258] The estimator shall not apply recycled parts to the collision estimate if no available parts are returned.
56. [R259] The adjuster shall review the collision estimate.
57. [R260] The adjuster shall request a recycled parts audit of the collision estimate.
58. [R261] The recycled parts search results provided to the estimator shall be retrieved by the system.
59. [R262] The system shall generate an audit report based on the available recycled parts and the collision estimate.
60. [R263] The audit report shall include the total number of recycled parts used in the estimate.
61. [R264] The audit report shall include the number of available recycled parts from the original search results.
62. [R265] The audit report shall include the percentage of available recycled parts used in the estimate
63. [R266] The audit report shall include the total score of the audit which sums the indivual line items.
64. [R267] The audit report shall be displayed to the user.
65. [R268] The audit report shall be available to other adjusters at later points in time.
66. [R269] The user shall select to view the preferred repair facility ratings.
67. [R270] The current repair facility ratings shall be displayed to the user.
68. [R271] The system will display a blank set of ratings if there are not ratings yet defined.
69. [R272] The user shall enter new ratings based on the audit report
70. [R273] The ratings shall be from a scale of 1-10
71. [R274] The ratings shall include categories for attempted use of recycled parts and actual use of recycled parts
72. [R275] The user shall select to save the preferred repair facility ratings.
73. [R276] The preferred repair facility ratings shall be saved by the system.
